description: Implement the selective subtree potential update applied after each pivot (O(subtree size), int64).
commit: feat: add selective subtree potential update
steps: Create potentials.go in the repo root (SPDX BSL-1.0, package mcf).

Implement `func (s *solverState) updatePotentials(subtreeRoot int, delta int64)`. Walks the portion of the thread starting at `subtreeRoot` up through `s.lastSucc[subtreeRoot]` inclusive, adding `delta` to `s.pi[node]` for each. Traversal: `node := subtreeRoot; stop := s.thread[s.lastSucc[subtreeRoot]]; for node != stop { s.pi[node] += delta; node = s.thread[node] }`. Document inline that the contiguous thread-range [subtreeRoot .. lastSucc[subtreeRoot]] is exactly the subtree in post-order.

Create potentials_test.go. Build a solverState by hand with n=5, root=5, a tree where node 1 is the root of a 3-node subtree {1, 2, 3} and nodes 0, 4 sit outside that subtree. Set thread/lastSucc consistently (thread threads nodes in post-order: for example thread = [4, 2, 3, 1, 0, ...], lastSucc[1] = 1 after traversal order). Populate pi = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 0]. Call updatePotentials(1, +7). Assert pi[1], pi[2], pi[3] all increased by 7 (now 27, 37, 47); pi[0] and pi[4] unchanged; pi[root] unchanged. Add TestSubtreeUpdateOnSingletonSubtree where subtreeRoot == lastSucc[subtreeRoot] (single-node subtree) and exactly one pi entry changes.
acceptance_criteria: updatePotentials mutates pi entries only within the contiguous thread-range [subtreeRoot .. lastSucc[subtreeRoot]].
